name: Peking Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Lard, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
FROM THE FRUGAL GOURMET THREE ANCIENT CUISINES COOKBOOK Basic Dough Recipe
for Boiled Dumplings and Peking Pancakes:
1. Mix the flour and salt in a bowl.
2. Gradually add the boiling water and stir with chopsticks until a dough forms.
3. Add the lard, knead all ingredients together on a plastic counter under a bowl for 20 minutes.
4. Roll the dough into a snake about 1 inch in diameter and cut into pieces 1 inch long.
5. Roll each piece into a ball and press out flat to make small pancakes.
6. Brush the top of one pancake with sesame oil and place another pancake on top of it.
7. Using a rolling pin, roll both pancakes together until they are about 5 inches in diameter. Add more flour as needed.
8. Place the stacked pancakes in a hot SilverStone-lined pan.
9. Cook until tiny brown spots form on the bottom for about 1 minute, then carefully turn the pancake over using a spatula.
10. Continue cooking the second side until fully cooked through.
11. Repeat with the remaining pancakes.
12. To reheat, place the cooled pancakes in a bamboo steamer and warm gently.
Note: Avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
